title: Neuromuscular blocking drugs
body_html:
Neuromuscular blocking drugs are mainly in surgery as an adjunct to anaesthetic agents. They cause muscle paralysis which is necessary prerequisite for mechanical ventilation.

Types of neuromuscular blocking drugs

DepolarizingNon-depolarizing
Mechanism of action Binds to nicotinic acetylcholine receptors resulting in persistent depolarization of the motor end plateCompetitive antagonist of nicotinic acetylcholine receptors
ExamplesSuccinylcholine (also known as suxamethonium)Tubcurarine, atracurium, vecuronium, pancuronium
Adverse effectsMalignant hyperthermia
Hyperkalaemia (normally transient)		Hypotension
ReversalAcetylcholinesterase inhibitors (e.g. neostigmine)
3
100
